title: 200th Episode Mailbag Spectacular: Our Dream Double Features and Francis Ford Coppola's Marvel Throwdown
format: The 200th episode: a listener mailbag on the Coppola-Scorsese Marvel feud, dream director-and-actor pairings, do-over movies, and double features, plus Sean's interview with Robert Eggers about The Lighthouse.

segments.heading: Coppola, Scorsese, and the Marvel throwdown

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Out this week. The Scorsese movie that keeps the 'Marvel isn't cinema' fight fresh. Sean and Amanda read Coppola's 'despicable' quote and James Gunn's Groot-and-Rocket Instagram reply.

films.title: The Wife
films.note: The reference point for 'what is 2019's The Wife': the awards movie no one can find or see.

films.title: The Two Popes
films.note: Amanda's pick for 2019's version: a Green Book buddy comedy about the Catholic Church she can't believe exists.

films.title: Harriet
films.note: Amanda took one for the team and saw it; Sean hasn't.

films.title: Queen & Slim
films.note: Asked why there isn't more buzz; they've seen it under embargo and won't podcast about it yet. Out around Thanksgiving.

films.title: Gemini Man
films.note: Not a success, but Sean admires it for chasing a new high frame rate instead of playing it safe.

films.title: Joker
films.note: The surprise Sean admits he didn't see coming; about to clear $900 million.

films.title: Vice
films.note: The 'here's the path for every nominee' exercise, with Vice as the one that was never going to win.

films.title: Wimbledon
films.note: Sean's dream sports-movie pitch: remake it, but good, with Sorkin writing.

films.title: Moneyball
films.note: Sean's favorite sports movie and the model he keeps reaching for.

films.title: The Firm
films.note: Sean's example of the old star economy: Chris Evans couldn't draw that kind of crowd to his version of it now.

films.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
films.note: As close as Jim Carrey got to the quiet, vulnerable register Sean wants PTA to pull out of him.

films.title: Punch-Drunk Love
films.note: The Adam Sandler transformation Sean wants a Carrey equivalent of.

films.title: Phantom Thread
films.note: Same idea: the PTA reinvention Sean would build around Carrey.

films.title: The Tragedy of Macbeth
films.note: Sean's long-held Coen-and-Denzel wish, arriving via Joel Coen's A24 Macbeth with Frances McDormand.

films.title: Hustlers
films.note: Cited as the $100M hit that makes Amanda's Nancy Meyers-and-J.Lo pairing feel plausible.

segments.heading: The movie that most affronts us

films.title: Murder on the Orient Express
films.note: Amanda's rant: Branagh grafts a non-canon dead-love backstory onto Poirot, then sets up a sequel Poirot could never actually solve because he arrives after the murder.

films.title: Death on the Nile
films.note: The sequel teased at the end, parked in the annual Venom/Joker October slot, which delights Sean.

films.title: Zeroville
films.note: Sean's pick: James Franco's adaptation of Steve Erickson's great New Hollywood novel, wrecked by the wrong tone despite a Blades-of-Glory-sized comic cast.

segments.heading: Top five do-over movies
segments.note: good premise, mishandled, and how they'd fix it

picks.title: Yesterday
picks.note: Great premise, great lead performance, didn't stick the landing. She wanted more.

picks.title: The Great Gatsby
picks.note: Keep Leo, keep Baz: it understood Lana Del Rey but not Gatsby.

picks.title: The Bling Ring
picks.note: From a place of love: Hustlers before Hustlers, without the follow-through; push the fame-and-delusion levers harder.

picks.title: Duplicity
picks.note: Keep Roberts and Owen; she still can't follow the last three-quarters.

picks.title: Molly's Game
picks.note: Keep everyone, skinny it down: lose the Idris Elba framing device, focus it into a week-in-the-life.

picks.title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
picks.note: Just let Lord and Miller finish their version; he doesn't care about Ron Howard's.

picks.title: Public Enemies
picks.note: A Michael Mann miss; try swapping Depp and Bale so Depp plays the lawman.

picks.title: Hancock
picks.note: Keep Will Smith and Charlize Theron, but make the R-rated Vince Gilligan-script version.

picks.title: Funny People
picks.note: Best first hour Apatow's ever made; redo the second half as a James Brooks movie, less Eric Bana and Leslie Mann.

segments.heading: The set we'd sit in on
segments.note: one entire production, start to finish

films.title: Ocean's Eleven
films.note: Amanda's only real answer: be the 12th on a fun Soderbergh set in the Clooney-Pitt-Damon prank era.

films.title: Eyes Wide Shut
films.note: Sean's answer: the long, painful, fracturing Kubrick shoot with Tom and Nicole, watched in real time.

films.title: Taxi Driver
films.note: The Joker pairing Amanda wants to program at the ArcLight.

films.title: Working Girl
films.note: Anchor of Amanda's cheating four-film run of working-woman comedies.

films.title: Baby Boom
films.note: Second in that run.

films.title: The Devil Wears Prada
films.note: Third, one of the two Nancy Meyers-adjacent entries.

films.title: The Intern
films.note: Closes out Amanda's set.

films.title: The Treasure of the Sierra Madre
films.note: First leg of Sean's greed triple bill.

films.title: Scarecrow
films.note: The great Gene Hackman movie in the middle Sean thinks people haven't seen.

films.title: There Will Be Blood
films.note: Sean's closer: three films in concert but not quite the same genre.

segments.heading: Best and worst Best Picture of the decade

films.title: Moonlight
films.note: Sean's best win by far, proof he doesn't actually know what Academy voters will do.

films.title: The Artist
films.note: Sean's worst win by far, a Weinstein movie, brutal campaign, good riddance.

films.title: The King's Speech
films.note: Amanda's single worst call, over The Social Network.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: Their best-movie-of-the-decade theory; they want the Fincher-Sorkin sequel.

films.title: 12 Years a Slave
films.note: Appreciated, if not their favorite of its year.

films.title: Argo
films.note: Enjoyed and appreciated.

films.title: Spotlight
films.note: Same, though Sean doesn't think it should have won.

films.title: Birdman
films.note: Sean's bigger problem win from a tough year.

films.title: The Lighthouse
films.note: The batshit, black-and-white two-hander with Pattinson and Dafoe, written with his brother Max in period coastal dialect, storyboarded shot for shot and, per Eggers, meant to be funny.

films.title: The Witch
films.note: The surprise hit that opened doors; Eggers picks control over scale every time. Black Phillip the goat was impossible; you can't train a goat.

films.title: At Eternity's Gate
films.note: Where Dafoe already had the post-Van Gogh beard he'd grow into a sea god's.

films.title: The Servant
films.note: Losey and Pinter: a two-hander this film is clearly indebted to.

films.title: The African Queen
films.note: Cited among the confined two-and-three-handers Eggers looked at.

films.title: Mirror
films.note: Tarkovsky's specificity made Eggers feel he'd had the same childhood: his lesson in the detail it takes to transport a viewer.

films.title: Persona
films.note: Bergman's two-hander, another with a foghorn motif.

films.title: The Vikings
films.note: The one Viking movie Eggers can name, beardless Kirk Douglas and all, teed up ahead of his own bearded Viking revenge project.

films.title: Waves
films.note: Eggers' answer to 'the last great thing you've seen': Trey Edward Shults turning his influences into something new.

films: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs

TV: The Lord of the Rings (Amazon series)

Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

The Treasure of the Sierra Madre.title: The Treasure of the Sierra Madre

Birdman.title: Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)

Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs.title: Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs
